title: MVCC Read Operation Algorithm
description: Pseudocode showing how MVCC performs a read operation using version visibility rules
code:
MVCC_READ(transaction_id, data_item):
BEGIN
 start_ts ← transaction_table[transaction_id].start_timestamp
 version ← get_latest_version(data_item)
 
 WHILE version ≠ NULL DO
 IF version.commit_timestamp ≤ start_ts AND
 version.transaction_id ≠ transaction_id THEN
 RETURN version.value
 END IF
 version ← version.previous_version
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN NULL // No visible version
END
explanation: The algorithm iterates through version chain to find the most recent version whose commit timestamp is earlier than the transaction's start timestamp, ensuring snapshot isolation.

complexity.time: O(V) where V is number of versions

title: MVCC Write Operation Algorithm
description: Pseudocode showing how MVCC creates a new version during write operations
code:
MVCC_WRITE(transaction_id, data_item, new_value):
BEGIN
 start_ts ← transaction_table[transaction_id].start_timestamp
 
 // Check for write-write conflict
 latest_version ← get_latest_version(data_item)
 IF latest_version.commit_timestamp > start_ts THEN
 ABORT_TRANSACTION(transaction_id)
 RETURN FAIL
 END IF
 
 // Create new version
 new_version ← ALLOCATE_VERSION()
 new_version.value ← new_value
 new_version.transaction_id ← transaction_id
 new_version.commit_timestamp ← NULL
 new_version.previous_version ← latest_version
 
 // Insert into version chain (uncommitted)
 INSERT_VERSION(data_item, new_version)
 
 RETURN SUCCESS
END
explanation: Write operation creates a new version but doesn't commit until transaction commits. It checks for conflicts with committed versions.

title: MVCC Transaction Commit
description: Pseudocode showing the commit process in MVCC with write-ahead logging
code:
MVCC_COMMIT(transaction_id):
BEGIN
 // Write WAL record first
 log_record ← CREATE_LOG_RECORD(transaction_id)
 WRITE_AHEAD_LOG(log_record)
 
 // Mark all versions created by this transaction as committed
 FOR EACH version IN transaction_versions[transaction_id] DO
 version.commit_timestamp ← CURRENT_SYSTEM_TIME()
 END FOR
 
 // Update transaction table
 transaction_table[transaction_id].status ← COMMITTED
 transaction_table[transaction_id].commit_timestamp ← CURRENT_SYSTEM_TIME()
 
 RETURN SUCCESS
END
explanation: Commit process uses write-ahead logging to ensure durability, then marks all versions as committed making them visible to other transactions.

complexity.time: O(V) where V is versions created by transaction

title: Timestamp Ordering Algorithm
description: Pseudocode for multiversion timestamp ordering concurrency control
code:
MV_TS_ORDERING(operation, transaction_id, data_item):
BEGIN
 ts ← transaction_table[transaction_id].timestamp
 
 IF operation = READ THEN
 read_ts[data_item] ← MAX(read_ts[data_item], ts)
 version ← FIND_VERSION(data_item, 
 max(write_ts[version] < ts))
 IF version = NULL THEN
 ABORT(transaction_id)
 ELSE
 RETURN version.value
 END IF
 
 ELSIF operation = WRITE THEN
 IF ts < read_ts[data_item] THEN
 ABORT(transaction_id) // Read before write violation
 END IF
 IF ts < write_ts[data_item] THEN
 // Thomas Write Rule: ignore
 RETURN SUCCESS
 END IF
 CREATE_NEW_VERSION(data_item, ts)
 write_ts[data_item] ← ts
 END IF
 
 RETURN SUCCESS
END
explanation: MV T/O uses transaction timestamps to order operations. It maintains read and write timestamps for each object and allows/denies operations based on timestamp ordering rules.

complexity.time: O(log n) for timestamp updates, O(V) for version search

title: Garbage Collection in MVCC
description: Pseudocode for identifying and cleaning obsolete versions
code:
MVCC_GARBAGE_COLLECT(data_item):
BEGIN
 // Find the earliest start timestamp among active transactions
 min_active_ts ← MIN(
 t.start_timestamp FOR t IN transaction_table 
 WHERE t.status = ACTIVE)
 
 // Versions committed before min_active_ts are obsolete
 current_version ← get_latest_version(data_item)
 
 WHILE current_version ≠ NULL DO
 next_version ← current_version.previous_version
 
 IF current_version.commit_timestamp < min_active_ts AND
 current_version.transaction_status ≠ ACTIVE THEN
 // This version is no longer needed
 REMOVE_VERSION(data_item, current_version)
 END IF
 
 current_version ← next_version
 END WHILE
END
explanation: GC identifies versions whose commit timestamp is older than all active transactions' start timestamps, meaning no future transaction can see them.
